zotero.abstractNote: The ability to decompose a complex task into simpler, reusable sub-tasks is a hallmark of human intelligence. In the context of deep learning, this suggests a modular architecture where different modules specialize in different sub-tasks. However, a key challenge is how to coordinate these modules to solve a given task. In this paper, we propose a novel architecture, the Global Workspace (GW), which acts as a communication bottleneck between a set of specialized modules. The GW allows modules to read from and write to a shared memory, enabling them to coordinate their actions. We show that our proposed architecture can learn to solve complex tasks by decomposing them into sub-tasks, and that the learned modules are reusable across different tasks. We evaluate our approach on a variety of tasks, including multi-task reinforcement learning and image classification, and show that it outperforms existing modular architectures.
zotero.conferenceName: The Tenth International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2022)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Tenth International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2022)
